notes.body:
Spoke - said they have an offer on the table. Contract sent via email - asked her what that is so I can check and see if we can beat that

Also talked about setting up a time and day where we can walk the prop

Said she's getting tired of people asking to walk the prop only to find out that they would be reselling it (wholesalers) - told her we're looking to either flip it or rent it out - just a matter of us doing our due diligence

Said she'll get w/ her sister and will get back to me

Offer made at 10K - wants something a bit higher than that since the prop has good bones - gave her the pitch on it - also brought up the fact that I spoke w/ my Capital partners about the 8 other houses she's working on and told her that if we can make a deal on this - we could probably make a deal on the other 8.

Said to let her mull it over and will get back to me - we're good
As per Mido -offer 10K

As per Mido - we have a buyer who wants to walk the prop on Monday --

Spole w/ Carla, said MONDAY is a stretch for her but can do the ff times:

  • 8:30 - 9am
    & 3pm

  • Also mentioned --
    she's got 8 properties she's working on that her husband left -some projects left unfinished. Needs help on either fixing it up or making a deal out of it
Spoke w/ Carla - went to the prop this weekend, cleaned & fixed some stuff up. Said she'll have everything that she needs over this weekend - FUP Early part next wk

Prop was a Tax sale - at least 7 yrs ago

asked if there's anything owed to it - said none and that it's free & clear

mentioned she had the grass cut on it and will be cleaning it out.

Also mentioned she had the Windows boarded up simply bec the city requires it

Asked if we'll be brokering it - told her we buy directly and either flip or rent it out

PRICE

Asked for her ball park figure - said she has no idea

Gave her a range on our offer sight unseen: 10K maybe 15K max - and asked her if that would get the deal done - said probably.

Told her would need to get the pics for us to better asses and figure out exactly what kind of work is needed - can send pics over the weekend

As per Mido:

MAO will be $18k
if it's a buy and hold deal
Will send to Lender - wait on Numbers - as per Adam

As per adam: Will send to a lender before we take further actions.

• Escrow will be w/ the Title Company

  • We have that assignment clause cause we get all the property under our mother company but after we lock it up we change it to a different LLC with that property address so we control the taxes once we rent it out

Right off the bat - said you guys sent me a wholesale contract and asked if we're wholesaling it or if we're buying it directly - told him we do a bit of both case by case - said if you guys are wholesaling this then forget about it - said he don't want to go through all that and then 6mos has passed he's got no money yet or we get back to him w/ a whole lot of changes. Said he's doing the same thing

  • Told him I know for a fact that we're buying this one - that's why we have GC's lined up for us to check and see w/c one gives us the best price.

  • Re: Access to the prop - said he lives near the area. Once we're locked up we can just hit him up and let him know who's coming or what and he can facilitate

Need to know the Escrow - where it's going to be deposited to or what not

Brought up the fact the he saw an "assignment fee clause"

APG Lead Summary
========================================
Updated: May 07, 2026 04:04 AM

--- Property ---
Address: 445 Cleveland Rd,, cleveland, OH 44108
Beds / Baths: 2 / 1.5
Sqft: 1385
Type: Single Family
Condition: Fair

--- Financials ---
Asking Price: $50,000
ARV (Claude+comps): $100,000
70% ARV (MAO): $70,000
Repairs Needed: Bathroom gutted, kitchen gutted, floors torn up in living/dining, roof needs replacement, garage roof partially down, couple windows may need replacement, full paint needed

--- Deal Context ---
Deal Type: Cash
Motivation: Investor looking to offload project
Timeline: No rush
Reason: Seller is an investor who started a flip/rental project but is open to selling before completing the work.
Lead Temp: Warm

--- Last Call Analysis (Claude) ---
Rating: 6/10
Summary: Seller Mike is an investor who purchased 445 Cleveland Road in Cleveland, Ohio (2 bed, 1.5 bath with basement and 3-car garage). He started gutting the property—bathroom, kitchen, and floors torn up—but hasn't reinstalled anything yet. The property has a brand new furnace and hot water heater (gas), copper plumbing, Romex wiring, and non-vinyl siding that just needs paint. The roof needs replacement and one section of the 3-car garage roof is down. Mike was planning to flip or rent it but is open to selling as-is for around $50k. Jeff committed to reviewing with capital partners and following up via text.

What we could improve:
  • Did not ask seller's bottom-line price or how firm he is o

Claude Call Rating + Action Items
======================================
Rating: 7/10
Lead Temperature: Warm

What could have been done better:
  • Did not ask seller's bottom-line price or flexibility on the $50k ask
  • Skipped questions about liens, mortgage balance, or how seller acquired the property
  • Did not ask about occupancy, tenant status, or whether property is vacant
  • Did not clarify seller's timeline or urgency — no specific callback date set
  • Missed opportunity to ask about comps or ARV — seller is an active flipper and likely knows local values

Action items:
  • Send seller company website and establish credibility via text
  • Pull comps for 445 Cleveland Road to estimate ARV and determine max offer
  • Run title search and check for liens or encumbrances
  • Verify property is vacant and assess holding costs given partial demo state
  • Prepare cash offer below $50k and present within 24-48 hours to maintain momentum
  • Follow up within 2 business days with formal offer or request for in-person walkthrough

Next steps from call: VA will text seller website for due diligence, discuss with capital partners, and follow up with offer.

Red flags: Seller hasn't turned water on yet — plumbing untested, Garage has roof damage on one side, Unclear siding material and condition, Property partially gutted — increased holding/carrying costs if deal delays

Offer made at 30K - declined. Said he'd rather fix it up he can get more than that. Asked for a price in the mid - said he can't go lower than 42K - asked for some pics - said he can send over a video - told him will wait on it and let him know

ARV is $85k-$90k. Needs a new roof, kitchen, flooring, paint, bathroom, windows, which I’m estimating about $30k. Investor will want to be all in $70k max. That leaves approx. 40k purchase price for investor which means we have to get him down to $30k absolute MAO
